processSteps.title: Send the Basics
processSteps.description: Share the equipment type, location, condition, photos, and any available specs or documentation.

processSteps.title: Market Review
processSteps.description: Petra reviews where the asset fits, who is likely to care, and whether brokerage makes sense.

processSteps.title: Position the Asset
processSteps.description: We organize the story around real field use, condition, logistics, and practical buyer questions.

processSteps.title: Connect Real Buyers
processSteps.description: The equipment is put in front of relevant buyers without running it through a public auction process.

requirements: Model, year, size, pressure, horsepower, capacity, or other core specs
Photos, serial plates, inspection notes, or maintenance records
Target timing, asking range, and logistics constraints

sellerBenefits.title: No Auction Guesswork
sellerBenefits.description: Petra focuses on direct buyer conversations, practical valuation, and controlled exposure.

sellerBenefits.title: Field-Aware Positioning
sellerBenefits.description: Listings are framed around how operators evaluate equipment: condition, fit, documents, and logistics.

sellerBenefits.title: Regional Buyer Network
sellerBenefits.description: We work across Wyoming, the Rockies, the Bakken, North Dakota, Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, and Montana.

faqs.question: What equipment can Petra help sell?
faqs.answer: Petra focuses on used oilfield and industrial assets such as compressors, separators, tank batteries, pump packages, production equipment, and regional surplus.

faqs.question: Do I need perfect documentation before reaching out?
faqs.answer: No. Photos, core specs, location, and condition notes are enough to start a review. Strong documentation helps position the asset more accurately.

faqs.question: Is this a public auction process?
faqs.answer: No. Petra is built around brokerage, direct buyer conversations, equipment positioning, and practical deal support.
